title: Solubility rules
definition: A short, ordered list of empirical rules that predict whether an ionic compound dissolves in water — with exceptions. They are *tabulated facts*, not theorems: nitrates and group-1 salts are soluble; most carbonates are not, except those of group-1 cations and ammonium. ChemKernel applies them in a fixed precedence and cites the governing rule, so a precipitate is classified, never asserted.
